manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: round, time, racket, trunk, ketchup, millionaire, capital, circle, back.
Your opponent's team words are: worm, tooth, torch, board, spy, ground, card, watch.
Distractor words are: eagle, olympus, lion.
The kill words are: suit, paper, post, missile, pipe.

1.  **Analyze the Request:**

    *   Output Format: Strict format required (`CLUE: <WORD>`, `TARGETS: <TARGETS>`). No extra text.

    *   Team Words: round, time, racket, trunk, ketchup, millionaire, capital, circle, back.
    *   Opponent Words: worm, tooth, torch, board, spy, ground, card, watch.
    *   Distractor Words: eagle, olympus, lion.
    *   Kill Words: suit, paper, post, missile, pipe.

2.  **Analyze Team Words:**

    *   time (clock, duration, era)
    *   racket (noise, sports equipment, fraud)
    *   trunk (elephant part, car storage, tree part)
    *   ketchup (condiment, tomato)
    *   millionaire (wealth, money)
    *   capital (city, money, letter)
    *   circle (shape, group)
    *   back (body part, return, support)

3.  **Identify Potential Associations:**

    *   *Car:* trunk, back (seat?), round (tire?), watch (clock?).
    *   *Letters/Writing:* capital, card (opponent), paper (kill), post (kill).
    *   *Time:* time, watch (opponent), round (duration?), back (ago?).
    *   *Tomato/Red:* ketchup. (Maybe millionaire? No.)
    *   *Elephant:* trunk. (Maybe lion (distractor), eagle (distractor)).
    *   *City/Country:* capital, ground (opponent), post (kill).
    *   *Sound:* racket, round (gunshot?).

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: round, time, racket, trunk, ketchup, millionaire, capital, circle, back.
Your opponent's team words are: worm, tooth, torch, board, spy, ground, card, watch.
Distractor words are: eagle, olympus, lion.
The kill words are: suit, paper, post, missile, pipe.

CLUE: LOOP
TARGETS: circle, round, time

The words circle, round, time were guessed correctly. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: racket, trunk, ketchup, millionaire, capital, back.
Remaining words for your opponent are: worm, tooth, torch, board, spy, ground, card, watch.
Remaining distractor words are: eagle, olympus, lion.
Remaining kill words are: suit, paper, post, missile, pipe.

message.reasoning:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Game: Codenames (or similar word association game).
    *   Role: Spymaster giving a clue.
    *   Input: Remaining team words, opponent words, distractors, kill words.
    *   Constraint: Single-word clue, comma-separated list of target words.
    *   Format:
        CLUE: <WORD>
        TARGETS: <TARGETS>
    *   Goal: Connect remaining team words without touching opponent, distractor, or kill words.
    *   Remaining Team Words: racket, trunk, ketchup, millionaire, capital, back.
    *   Opponent Words: worm, tooth, torch, board, spy, ground, card, watch.
    *   Distractors: eagle, olympus, lion.
    *   Kill Words: suit, paper, post, missile, pipe.

2.  **Evaluate Remaining Team Words:**
    *   racket (sports, noise, tennis)
    *   trunk (elephant, car, tree, luggage)
    *   ketchup (condiment, tomato, red)
    *   millionaire (rich, money, wealth)
    *   capital (money, city, uppercase)
    *   back (body part, return, reverse)

3.  **Identify Potential Connections:**
    *   *Money/Wealth:* millionaire, capital. (Strong link)
    *   *Body Parts:* back, trunk (elephant nose?), tooth (opponent). "Back" and "Trunk" could be body parts (human back, elephant trunk), but "tooth" is opponent.
    *   *Sports:* racket, back (backhand?), ground (opponent), board (opponent).
    *   *Storage/Container:* trunk (car/chest), capital (no), ketchup (bottle).
    *   *Red:* ketchup, capital (no), millionaire (no).
    *   *Top/Upper:* capital (city/top), top (not in list), back (reverse).
    *   *Return:* back, return (not in list).
    *   *Tree/Nature:* trunk (tree), ground (opponent), worm (opponent).
    *   *Car:* trunk, hood (not in list), wheel (not in list).
    *   *City/State:* capital, state (not in list).

4.  **Analyze Risks (Opponent/Distractor/Kill):**
    *   *Money link (millionaire, capital):*
        *   Clue: MONEY.
        *   Risks: card (opponent - credit card?), watch (opponent - expensive?), suit (kill - business suit?).
        *   "Suit" is a kill word. "Money" might lead to "Suit" (business).
        *   "Capital" can mean money or city. "Millionaire" is definitely money.
    *   *Body link (back, trunk):*
        *   Clue: BODY.
        *   Risks: tooth (opponent). This is risky.
    *   *Tree/Nature link (trunk):*
        *   Clue: TREE.
        *   Risks: ground (opponent), worm (opponent), lion (distractor), eagle (distractor).
    *   *Sports link (racket):*
        *   Clue: TENNIS.
        *   Risks: board (opponent - scoreboard?), ground (opponent - court?), watch (opponent).
    *   *Container link (trunk, ketchup?):*
        *   Weak link.
    *   *Upper/Top link (capital, back?):*
        *   Weak.

5.  **Focus on "Millionaire" and "Capital":**
    *   Both relate to finance/wealth/cities.
    *   "Capital" can mean wealth or city. "Millionaire" means wealth.
    *   Clue: WEALTH.
        *   Targets: millionaire, capital.
        *   Risks: card (credit card?), watch (luxury?), suit (kill - business attire). "Suit" is a kill word. Avoid clues that strongly suggest business attire.
    *   Clue: RICH.
        *   Targets: millionaire, capital.
        *   Risks: suit (kill).
    *   Clue: BANK.
        *   Targets: millionaire, capital.
        *   Risks: card (opponent), watch (no), suit (kill).
    *   Clue: FUND.
        *   Targets: millionaire, capital.
        *   Risks: card, suit.

6.  **Focus on "Trunk" and "Back":**
    *   Trunk (elephant), Back (human).
    *   Trunk (car), Back (car seat?).
    *   Trunk (tree), Back (support?).
    *   Clue: ELEPHANT.
        *   Targets: trunk.
        *   Risks: lion (distractor), eagle (distractor). Too specific for one word maybe.
    *   Clue: CAR.
        *   Targets: trunk.
        *   Risks: watch (no), board (dashboard?), ground (parking?).

If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

    *   *ketchup* + *trunk*? (Heinz? Bottle? Not strong.)

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: round, time, racket, trunk, ketchup, millionaire, capital, circle, back.
Your opponent's team words are: worm, tooth, torch, board, spy, ground, card, watch.

The kill words are: suit, paper, post, missile, pipe.
